mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid quarterly increase in the unemployment rate exceeds 0.5 percentage points, signaling an imminent labor market contraction that reduces aggregate corporate revenues and forces negative equity repricing over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms synchronized consumer demand strength, driving immediate positive earnings revisions and generating positive forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 5-day returns in > 55% of occurrences over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which supports corporate profit margins and yields positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth fails to predict positive returns when inflation exceeds 5% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation above 225.0 coincides with rising policy rates, forcing the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive real yields that compress equity discount factors and produce negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in > 65% of high-inflation/rising-rate regimes over a 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month gains in total nonfarm payrolls indicate robust labor market expansion, which sustains consumer spending momentum and generates positive equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows zero predictive power for 5-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01) across all macro regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falls below 1.5 percent on a quarterly basis, confirming a demand contraction that triggers recession risk pricing and forces negative equity repricing over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in > 70% of low-GDP-growth quarters over a 10-year evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in industrial production and rise in the unemployment rate confirms a broad-based economic slowdown that reduces corporate output and earnings, driving negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint negative signal fails to predict negative returns when the VIX index remains below 15.
